name: Leading-edge logic nodes (TSMC N3 → A14)
severityNote: TSMC single-source for AI; Samsung + Intel not at qualification
description: TSMC's N3P / N3X / N2 / A16 / A14 family is the only volume foundry path for the most advanced AI accelerator silicon. Samsung's GAA 2nm is technically available but not customer-qualified at NVIDIA / AMD volumes; Intel 18A is the closest second-source watch item but pre-HVM. The constraint is single-source qualification, not capacity in absolute terms.
stackPosition: Mask design → tape-out (Synopsys/Cadence tools) → photomask (Toppan/DNP/Hoya) → fab (TSMC F18/F18A/F22, Samsung S5, Intel D1X/Fab 52) → wafer sort → dicing → bin → ship to packaging. The fab stage is the visible chokepoint but the binding upstream constraint is mask qualification + foundry process release schedules, not silicon wafer supply per se.

supplyStructure.name: Samsung Foundry (GAA 2nm)
supplyStructure.note: GAA technology lead but customer qualification gap

supplyStructure.name: Intel Foundry (18A, 14A)
supplyStructure.note: 18A HVM ramp the binary event for foundry strategy

indicators.name: TSMC wafer revenue by technology + AI/HPC mix + N2 ramp commentary
Samsung Foundry segment results + GAA customer commentary
VLSI Symposium / IEDM accelerator process papers

companies.name: TSMC
companies.note: The pure-play on leading-edge logic; pricing power compounds each node

companies.name: Samsung Electronics
companies.note: GAA technology lead; foundry qualification gap is the discount

companies.name: Intel
companies.note: 18A foundry customer wins drive the entire thesis

companies.name: ASML
companies.note: Cross-reference — every leading-edge wafer requires EUV; High-NA is the next-node enabler

companies.name: Synopsys / Cadence
companies.note: EDA tools — every tape-out passes through both

companies.name: Tokyo Electron / Applied Materials / Lam
companies.note: WFE — leading-edge wafer step counts are 1.6× more than 7nm

companies.name: Rapidus
companies.note: Japanese 2nm foundry; METI + IBM co-investment, Hokkaido pilot 2027 target
companies.investors: METI, Toyota, Sony, Kioxia, NTT, SoftBank

insight: Leading-edge logic is the highest-quality structural long in the AI capex chain — TSMC's effective monopoly compounds across nodes and the customer concentration is now durable through 2030. The cleanest expression is TSM directly. The contrarian trade is Intel Foundry on 18A: pre-HVM, customer disclosures are sparse, but a single hyperscaler design win would re-rate the entire stock. Samsung Foundry is the perpetual disappointment — process is good, execution and customer relationships are not. Watch the China-domestic foundry path (SMIC N+2/N+3) — capable but export-controlled out of frontier AI silicon.

debates.question: Does Intel 18A achieve >$5B in foundry revenue from external customers by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Possible if Microsoft / a hyperscaler ASIC commits; consensus closer to $3B

debates.question: Does Samsung Foundry gain a flagship NVIDIA or AMD AI design by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Unlikely for flagship; possible for tertiary product or networking ASIC

debates.question: Does TSMC's AI / HPC revenue mix exceed 65% of total wafer revenue by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Almost certain on current trajectory; the question is upside not direction
